reviews.english: A broad piece of defensive armor, held in hand, formerly in general use in war, for the protection of the body.

example.english: Austrian police forced them back with batons, shields and pepper spray.

quality.back_translation: The Austrian police forced them back with batons, shields and pepper spray.

reviews.english: The quality of being shy; a fear of social interactions.

example.english: Her mother encouraged her to try community theater to overcome her shyness.

quality.back_translation: Her mother encouraged her to try a community theatre performance to overcome her shyness.

reviews.english: A situation or state of affairs.

quality.back_translation: One of the most important social structures is the state of society.

reviews.english: Exhibiting superconductivity.

example.english: At very low temperatures, this phase also becomes superconducting.

quality.back_translation: At very low temperatures, this phase also becomes superconductor.

reviews.english: (agriculture, ecology) Development which seeks to produce sustainable economic growth while ensuring future generations' ability to do the same by not exceeding the regenerative capacity of nature.

example.english: It aims to increase the impact of scientific research on sustainable development.

quality.machine_chinese: 其目的是增加科学研究对可持续发展的影响。
quality.back_translation: Its objective is to increase the impact of scientific research on sustainable development.

reviews.english: Of, or relating to synthesis.

example.english: Many synthetic socks are treated with chemicals to help reduce odor.

quality.back_translation: Many synthetic socks are treated with chemicals to help reduce the smell of their wearing.

reviews.english: The science and technology of the communication of messages over a distance using electric, electronic or electromagnetic impulses. [1932]

example.english: Banking, financial services, insurance and telecommunications were also opened up to foreign investment.

reviews.english: Duration of officeholding, or its limit; period in office of fixed length.

reviews.english: The study of God, a god, or gods; and of the truthfulness of religion in general. [from mid-14th c.]

reviews.english: (military) A cylindrical explosive projectile that can travel underwater and is used as a weapon. [from 1805]

reviews.english: (star) The dim, highly dense core remnant that is the final stage in the evolution of a star of low or medium mass, after nuclear reactions have ceased.
